reasoning: The reference value of 3.167 implies a base rate of roughly 2.167 'Strategic developments' per 30-day period in Hungary over the past year. This would mean more strategic developments occurring in just one month than normally occur in over a year. Hungary is politically stable; while polarization is high ahead of the 2026 elections, the vast majority of unrest takes the form of peaceful protests (coded as 'Protests'), not 'Strategic developments' (which include arrests, military deployments, or looted property). Reaching an average of over 1 'Strategic development' per day for a month is essentially unheard of in contemporary Hungary, barring extreme macro-shocks (e.g., warfare, total societal collapse, or a radical methodology shift by ACLED). I assign the minimum credible probability.
